questions.stem:
-- Where is your father?
-- He _____ Shanghai. He will be back next week.
questions.explanation: has been to 表示'去过某地（已回来）'，has gone to 表示'去了某地（还没回来）'。爸爸不在这里，下周才回来，说明他去了上海还没回来，用 has gone to。has been in 表示'一直待在某地'，不符合语境。

questions.stem: Do you know _____ the school sports meeting will be held?
questions.explanation: 宾语从句中缺少时间状语，问的是运动会什么时候举行，所以用 when 引导。that 不做成分但从句不缺成分时用；what 做主语或宾语；who 指人。这里从句 the school sports meeting will be held 结构完整，只缺时间，选 when。

questions.stem: The girl _____ is reading under the tree is my cousin.
questions.explanation: 定语从句中先行词是 the girl（人），关系代词在从句中作主语（is reading 的主语），所以用 who。which 用于修饰物；what 不引导定语从句；whom 在从句中作宾语，这里需要作主语，不能用 whom。

questions.stem: If it _____ tomorrow, we will go hiking in the mountains.
questions.explanation: if 引导的真实条件状语从句遵循'主将从现'原则：主句用一般将来时(will go)，从句用一般现在时。tomorrow 是将来时间，但 if 从句中不能用 will，所以选 doesn't rain（一般现在时否定）。

questions.stem: _____ useful advice he gave us! We all learned a lot.
questions.explanation: 感叹句结构：What + (a/an) + adj. + n.! 或 How + adj./adv.! 这里 advice 是不可数名词，不能加 a/an，所以用 What useful advice（What + adj. + 不可数名词）。How 后面直接加形容词，不加名词。

questions.stem: The young man is very _____. He always comes up with new ideas. (create)
questions.explanation: 空格前有 very，后面是句号，需要一个形容词作表语。create 是动词'创造'；creative 是形容词'有创造力的'；creation 是名词'创造物'；creator 是名词'创造者'。very 修饰形容词，选 creative。

questions.stem: We should _____ action to protect endangered animals before it is too late.
questions.explanation: take action 是固定搭配，意思是'采取行动'。make 搭配 decision/mistake；do 搭配 homework/housework；have 搭配 fun/a good time。注意固定动词搭配的积累。

questions.stem: Last month, our school held a special art competition. Students could use anything from nature to create their works. At first, I had no (8)_____ what to make.
questions.explanation: have no idea 是固定搭配，意思是'不知道，没有想法'。根据上下文，学校举办自然材料艺术比赛，'我'一开始不知道做什么。interest'兴趣'、courage'勇气'、chance'机会'都不符合'不知道做什么'的语境。

questions.stem: My friend Lily suggested collecting fallen leaves. 'They have such beautiful colours!' she said (9)_____. Her excitement was catching.
questions.explanation: 从 Lily 说的话'它们有好漂亮的颜色！'和后一句'她的兴奋具有感染力'可以推断她说话时很兴奋(excitedly)。sadly'悲伤地'、angrily'生气地'、nervously'紧张地'都与语境不符。注意利用后文 excitement 反推情绪词。

questions.stem: We spent the whole afternoon in the park, picking up leaves of different shapes and sizes. I was surprised to find that even the (10)_____ leaves on the ground could become art.
questions.explanation: 落叶掉在地上已经干枯了，dry leaves'干枯的叶子'最合语境。前文说 fallen leaves（落叶），地上的落叶是干的。fresh'新鲜的'、green'绿色的'与落叶矛盾；large'大的'没有对比意义。跨句推理：fallen → ground → dry。

questions.stem: On the day of the exhibition, many students (11)_____ around our artwork and took photos. Some teachers said it was the most creative piece they had ever seen.
questions.explanation: 根据语境，展览日很多同学围在作品旁拍照，gathered around'聚集在周围'最合适。practised'练习'、argued'争论'、competed'竞争'都不符合展览日同学围观拍照的场景。注意 gather around 是常见搭配。

questions.stem: Although we didn't win first prize, I learned an important (12)_____: beauty is everywhere if you look carefully enough.
questions.explanation: learn a lesson 是固定搭配，意思是'学到一个教训/道理'。后面'美无处不在，只要你仔细观察'是一个人生道理。skill'技能'、rule'规则'、habit'习惯'都不搭配 learn...from experience 的语境。

questions.stem: According to the passage, what is the main problem with plastic cutlery?
options: A. It is too expensive to produce.
B. It takes hundreds of years to break down.
C. It is difficult to use for eating.
D. It cannot be recycled at all.
questions.explanation: 原文说 plastic forks, spoons, and knives...take hundreds of years to decompose，即塑料餐具需要数百年才能分解。A 文中未提及成本高；C 不符合常识；D 文中说的是难以分解，不是完全不能回收。做细节题要回文定位关键信息。
questions.group_passage:
Every year, billions of plastic forks, spoons, and knives are thrown away after just one use. Most of them end up in landfills or oceans, where they take hundreds of years to decompose. This has led some inventors to come up with a surprising solution: cutlery you can eat.

Edible cutlery is made from natural ingredients like wheat flour, rice, and corn. After you finish your meal, you can simply eat the spoon or fork. If you choose not to eat it, no worries -- it will break down naturally in just a few days, unlike plastic, which pollutes the environment for centuries.

The idea first became popular in India, where a company called Bakeys started producing edible spoons in 2010. The founder, Narayana Peesapaty, was worried about the amount of plastic waste in his country. He spent years testing different recipes until he found one that was strong enough to hold hot soup without getting soft too quickly.

Today, edible cutlery comes in many flavours, including chocolate, vanilla, and even spicy options. Some restaurants in Europe and Asia have started using them. However, the cost is still higher than plastic -- an edible spoon costs about three times more. Supporters believe that as production increases, prices will drop.

Critics point out that edible cutlery alone cannot solve the plastic problem. But Peesapaty argues that every small step matters. 'If we can replace even 10% of plastic cutlery,' he says, 'that is billions of pieces of plastic saved from the ocean.'

questions.stem: Why did Narayana Peesapaty spend years testing different recipes?
options: A. He wanted to make the spoons taste like chocolate.
B. He needed to find a recipe that could hold hot food.
C. He tried to make the cheapest spoons possible.
D. He wanted to open a restaurant in India.
questions.explanation: 原文说 He spent years testing different recipes until he found one that was strong enough to hold hot soup without getting soft too quickly。推断他反复试验是为了找到能承受热食物的配方。A 文中巧克力是后来的口味选择，不是他试验的目的；C 和 D 文中未提及。推理题要根据原文细节推进一步。

questions.stem: The word 'decompose' in Paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to _____.
questions.explanation: decompose 意为'分解、腐烂'。上下文说塑料餐具被丢弃后在垃圾填埋场或海洋中需要数百年才能 decompose，后文也用 break down naturally 描述可食用餐具的分解过程。break down = decompose'分解'。做词义猜测题要在上下文找同义替换线索。

questions.stem: What is Peesapaty's attitude towards solving the plastic problem?
options: A. He thinks it is completely hopeless.
B. He believes every small change can help.
C. He thinks only governments can solve it.
D. He is not interested in the problem any more.
questions.explanation: 原文最后一段引用 Peesapaty 的话：every small step matters，If we can replace even 10%...that is billions of pieces saved。他认为每一小步都有意义，即使只替换10%也能拯救数十亿塑料。所以他的态度是积极的——每个小改变都有帮助。做观点态度题关注人物直接引语。

options: A. They can also learn to be responsible for another life.
B. The community centre held a Pet Adoption Day last Saturday.
C. Volunteers helped match each family with the right pet.
D. Some children were so excited that they couldn't stop smiling.
E. The event was organised to raise money for a new library.
questions.explanation: 第一段开头需要引出主题——社区宠物领养日活动。B 句'上周六社区中心举办了宠物领养日'是全文的引入句，交代了时间、地点和事件。其他选项都是后续细节，不适合放在文章开头。
questions.group_passage:
(17)_____ More than fifty cats and dogs were looking for new homes. The hall was decorated with colourful posters that read 'Give Me a Home.'

Families from the neighbourhood came early in the morning. (18)_____ A little boy named Jack fell in love with a brown puppy at first sight. 'Can we take him home, Mum?' he asked.

(19)_____ They explained how much food each animal needed, how often to take dogs for walks, and what to do if a pet got sick. This way, every animal could find a suitable family.

The event was a big success -- thirty-two animals found new homes that day. For children like Jack, having a pet is more than just fun. (20)_____ As Jack said on his way home, hugging his new puppy, 'I promise I will take good care of you.'

options: A. They can also learn to be responsible for another life.
B. The community centre held a Pet Adoption Day last Saturday.
C. Volunteers helped match each family with the right pet.
D. Some children were so excited that they couldn't stop smiling.
E. The event was organised to raise money for a new library.
questions.explanation: 第二段描述家庭早早来到现场，后文提到小男孩 Jack 一见钟情一只小狗。D 句'有些孩子兴奋得停不下来笑'衔接了家庭到来后孩子们的反应，与后文 Jack 的表现形成连贯叙述。C 是志愿者匹配的内容，放在第三段更合适。

options: A. They can also learn to be responsible for another life.
B. The community centre held a Pet Adoption Day last Saturday.
C. Volunteers helped match each family with the right pet.
D. Some children were so excited that they couldn't stop smiling.
E. The event was organised to raise money for a new library.
questions.explanation: 第三段后文详细描述了喂食量、遛狗频率、宠物生病怎么办等匹配工作的细节。C 句'志愿者帮助每个家庭匹配合适的宠物'是这些细节的总起句，逻辑最通顺。They explained 中的 They 指代 volunteers。

options: A. They can also learn to be responsible for another life.
B. The community centre held a Pet Adoption Day last Saturday.
C. Volunteers helped match each family with the right pet.
D. Some children were so excited that they couldn't stop smiling.
E. The event was organised to raise money for a new library.
questions.explanation: 最后一段说养宠物不仅仅是乐趣(more than just fun)，后面需要补充'还能怎样'。A 句'他们还能学会对另一个生命负责'承接 more than just fun，与后文 Jack 说的'我保证好好照顾你'形成呼应。E 是干扰项，与全文宠物领养主题无关。
